name: Quantification Questions
summary: Questions designed to turn vague pain into measurable business impact such as revenue, time, risk, headcount, or cost.
whenToUseIt: Use quantification questions once a buyer has admitted a problem and you need to build urgency, defend value, or prepare a business case that will hold up internally.
steps: Identify the process or problem that is currently creating friction.
Ask for scale, frequency, and consequence in business terms such as hours, dollars, delays, or error rates.
Translate the answer into an outcome that can support priority and investment.
example: A buyer says proposal generation is messy. You ask how many proposals go out per month, how long each one takes, and what happens when quotes are delayed. The issue becomes a measurable productivity and deal-speed problem.
